sanskrit:
श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात्।
स्वधर्मे निधनं श्रेयः परधर्मो भयावहः॥
transliteration:
śreyān sva-dharmo viguṇaḥ para-dharmāt sv-anuṣṭhitāt
sva-dharme nidhanaṁ śreyaḥ para-dharmo bhayāvahaḥ
literal_meaning: Better is one’s own duty, though imperfect, than the duty of another well-performed; death in one’s own duty is better, the duty of another is fraught with danger.
contextual_meaning: Authentic self-alignment outweighs perfectionistic imitation; psychological safety lies in congruence with one’s innate nature rather than borrowed roles.
psychological_state: Arjuna oscillates between social comparison and existential dread, fearing that his warrior identity is flawed yet dreading the dissonance of adopting a pacifist mask.
trigger_scenario: A software engineer from a modest background is offered a high-status sales job at a glamorous startup; the pay is triple but the role demands extroversion that feels fake, triggering panic attacks and imposter fears.
anchor_text: Stay true to your nature; imitating others invites inner danger.
